steps.title: Identify the clause structure and function
steps.content: The phrase we know that introduces a noun clause that functions as the object of the verb know. Since the main sentence is a statement, the noun clause must represent a fact being stated, not a question being asked.

steps.title: Apply the rule for noun clauses
steps.content:
A noun clause introduced by that uses declarative word order (subject before verb) and statement punctuation (period), not interrogative word order or question marks. Choice B correctly uses declarative word order—“the author's real name was Anne Brontë”—and ends with a period. The other choices violate this rule by using inverted word order (A and C) or ending with a question mark (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
